data.title: Baxter International names Andrew Hider CEO, separates CEO and Chair; targets ~3.0x leverage by 2026
data.description:
Baxter International appointed Andrew Hider as President & CEO, separated the CEO and Chair roles, and set a target to reach ~3.0x net leverage by end of 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • Andrew Hider appointed President, CEO and board member after a robust search overseen by independent directors.
  • Board leadership restructured: Brent Shafer becomes Non-Executive Chair to separate Chair and CEO duties.
  • Anita Zielinski named interim CFO as company conducts search for a permanent CFO following March 2026 departure.
  • Company completed strategic initiatives including divestiture of Kidney Care (Vantive) in Jan 2025 and reports FY2025 revenue of $11.2B.
  • Financial priorities: target net leverage ~3.0x by end of 2026, maintain investment-grade rating, prioritize debt repayment and growth-focused capex.

Original SEC Filing: BAXTER INTERNATIONAL INC [ BAX ] - 8-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: AES Modifies Credit and L/C Facilities With Citibank, SMBC and Barclays for Pending Ownership Change
data.description:
AES amended three financing arrangements to update change-of-control provisions tied to its announced merger. The amendments, completed with Citibank and participating lenders, Sumitomo Mitsui Banking and its lenders, and Barclays Bank, permit direct or indirect ownership by Global Infrastructure Management, EQT Fund Management, Qatar Investment Authority, and affiliated vehicles. The updates are intended to prevent default triggers, preserve borrowing and letter-of-credit capacity, and support liquidity through closing and post-transaction integration.

Agreement 1: AES Amends Citibank-Led Credit Facility to Permit Ownership by Global Infrastructure, EQT and QIA

  • Agreement type: Amendment No. 2 to revolving credit agreement
  • Counterparty: Citibank, as administrative agent, and lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 13 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Align change-of-control terms with pending ownership by new sponsors

Agreement 2: AES Updates SMBC-Led Credit Agreement for Change-of-Control Provisions Ahead of Take-Private

  • Agreement type: First Amendment to credit agreement
  • Counterparty: Sumitomo Mitsui Banking, as administrative agent, and lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 16 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Permit new ownership structure without triggering defaults

Agreement 3: AES Amends Barclays Letter of Credit Agreement to Accommodate New Sponsor Ownership

  • Agreement type: Amendment to letter of credit agreement
  • Counterparty: Barclays Bank
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 16 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Maintain L/C capacity through change of control

Original SEC Filing: AES CORP [ AES ] - 8-K - Mar. 19, 2026

data.title: Knot Offshore Partners Says Buyout Talks Have Ended
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Knot Offshore Partners talks about an offer from Knutsen NYK Offshore Tankers to buy the company for $10 a share in cash have been terminated.

The company said a committee of its board composed of directors who are unaffiliated with Knutsen had retained independent legal and financial advisors to evaluate the offer and engaged with the company about the proposal.

Following discussions between the two sides it was determined that they would not be able to reach an agreement on a deal and decided to end the talks, Knot said Thursday.

Shares fell 4.5% to $9.78 in after-hours trading. The stock, which closed the market session down less than 1% at $10.24, is up 82% in the past year.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Alumis Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Alumis Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 94 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -23 cents. The mean expectation of ten analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 98 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $-1.11 to -79 cents per share.

  • Revenue was $1.93 million​; analysts expected $2.38 million.

  • Alumis Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 94 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $92.78 million.

  • Alumis Inc shares had risen by 160.5% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 3.4% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the biotechnology & medical research peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Alumis Inc is $39.00, about 34.8% above its last closing price of $25.42

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data March 19 at 08:53 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Kimball Electronics publishes 2025 Guiding Principles Report; Scope 1&2 emissions down 9.3%, 37% renewable power
data.description:
Kimball Electronics released its 2025 Guiding Principles Report highlighting a 9.3% decline in Scope 1 and 2 emissions and progress toward net-zero and renewable energy targets.

Key Highlights:

  • Published 2025 Guiding Principles Report detailing ESG progress and aligned with ESRS, GRI, SASB, SDGs, UNGC and TCFD.
  • Climate action: Scope 1 and 2 emissions fell 9.3% YoY, advancing a 42% absolute reduction goal by 2030 and net-zero by 2050.
  • Renewables: Renewable electricity reached 37%, a 12% YoY increase toward a 2030 goal of 100% renewable power.
  • Waste & water: Achieved 92% beneficial use of waste and 12% recycled water; hazardous waste intensity down 13% from 2024.
  • Safety, governance & community: 22% reduction in TRIR, executives earned Above Threshold multiplier, and charitable giving hit $325,000 (115% of target).

Original SEC Filing: Kimball Electronics, Inc. [ KE ] - 8-K - Mar. 19, 2026

data.title: electroCore 10-K: $32.0M Revenue, $(13.97)M Net Loss
data.description:
electroCore reported fiscal 2025 net sales of $32.0 million and a net loss of approximately $(13.97) million, with operating loss of $(13.2) million. Revenue rose 27% year-over-year driven by increased U.S. prescription sales and growth in the Truvaga wellness channel, while gross margin expanded to about 87%. The results reflect higher sales to the U.S. VA and continued investment in commercial and R&D initiatives for next-generation products.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $32.0 million, up $6.9 million (27%) versus 2024.
  • Gross profit: $27.8 million, increased $6.4 million versus 2024; gross margin ~87% (2025) vs. 85% (2024).
  • Loss from operations: $(13.2) million, a decrease of $(1.0) million versus 2024.
  • Net loss: $(14.0) million (reported as $(13.966) million).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Increased prescription gammaCore sales in the U.S., notably through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), and stronger Truvaga wellness product sales.
  • Channel concentration: The U.S. government channel (VA) became the dominant channel, accounting for 71% of 2025 revenue via FSS and partner contracts.
  • Brand and product momentum: Continued commercial traction for prescription gammaCore and Quell Fibromyalgia, alongside growth in Truvaga wellness offerings.
  • Geographic and channel mix: Expansion in international channels including NHS cluster headache activity and ongoing direct-to-consumer wellness sales, which remain smaller contributors compared with U.S. prescription sales.
  • Operational focus and outlook: Increased investment in sales and marketing and in R&D for next-generation gammaCore devices and supporting app, with emphasis on managed care adoption to support future growth.

Original SEC Filing: electroCore, Inc. [ ECOR ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Harvard Apparatus Regenerative Technology 10-K: $0.70M revenue, $(0.42) EPS
data.description:
Harvard Apparatus Regenerative Technology reported product revenue of $0.704 million for the year, a 64% increase driven by expanded distribution and new supplement launches, while net loss narrowed to $(6.869) million, or $(0.42) per share on a weighted average of 16.52 million shares. Gross profit was $0.058 million with margin compression tied to a shift to distributor sales, and operating loss totaled $(6.915) million reflecting $7.619 million of operating expenses. Management flagged limited cash runway into Q2 2026 that could force cuts affecting clinical trial pace and commercialization efforts.

Financial Highlights

  • Product revenue: $0.704 million, up 64% year-over-year.
  • Gross profit: $0.058 million (margin compressed due to shift to distributor sales; cost of sales $0.646M).
  • Operating loss: $(6.915) million, reflecting total operating expenses of $7.619 million.
  • Net loss: $(6.869) million, improved 11% versus prior year net loss of $(7.732) million.
  • Net loss per common share, basic and diluted: $(0.42); weighted average shares outstanding: 16.52 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Expanded distributor network and new consumer health SKUs (including CoQ‑10 and sleep gummies) alongside core products such as Liver Guard contributed to sales gains.
  • Channel strategy shift: Company moved from direct sales to a distributor model, reducing marketing spend but altering gross margins and cost structure.
  • Clinical progress: Activated first esophageal clinical trial site in Q3 2023 with CRO IQVIA; recruitment delays persist, slowing trial advancement.
  • Operational risk / outlook: Limited cash runway into Q2 2026 may require operational cuts that could slow clinical trial enrollment and commercialization activities.

Original SEC Filing: Harvard Apparatus Regenerative Technology, Inc. [ HRGN ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Governments worldwide shield households from rising energy costs
data.description:
Governments worldwide are trying to shield consumers from soaring energy costs resulting from the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

Here’s how different countries are responding:

INDIA

** India, the world's fourth-largest refiner, will review its fuel exports if needed to ensure availability in the local markets, a government official said.

** India is assessing fuel-supply requests from its neighbours and will approve exports only if it has surplus volumes, the foreign ministry said on Wednesday.

** The country has barred consumers with piped natural gas from retaining, obtaining or refilling domestic liquefied petroleum gas cylinders.

** It has invoked emergency powers and directed refiners to maximise production of LPG, widely used for cooking. It cut sales to industry to avoid a shortage for 333 million homes with LPG connections.

GERMANY

** Germany's Finance Minister is examining ways to relieve consumers from rising fuel prices, including introducing a windfall tax on oil companies.

SOUTH KOREA

** South Korea is easing limits on coal-fired power generation capacity and raising nuclear power plant utilisation to as high as 80%.

** It is considering additional energy vouchers to support vulnerable households.

CHINA

** China has banned refined fuel exports to pre-empt a potential domestic fuel shortage, four sources said.

** It is also releasing fertiliser supplies from national commercial reserves ahead of spring planting.

AUSTRALIA

** Prime Minister Anthony Albanese urged Australians to avoid panic buying of petrol and diesel, which he said had led to shortages in some rural regions.

** The government last week announced it would release petrol and diesel from domestic reserves to ease supply‑chain disruptions linked to shortages in rural areas.

JAPAN

** Japan has asked Australia, its biggest supplier of liquefied natural gas, to boost output.

EUROPEAN UNION

** The European Commission, the EU executive, is instructing governments to be flexible when enforcing EU rules on gas imports, given concerns that strict compliance could delay LNG deliveries needed to stabilise supplies.

ITALY

** Italy has set aside some 417.4 million euros ($478 million) to cut excise duties on fuels until April 7, a decree approved by the cabinet showed.

CAMBODIA

** Cambodia is importing more fuel from suppliers in Singapore and Malaysia to make up for supply shortfalls from Vietnam and China.

MALAYSIA

** Malaysia will raise spending on petrol subsidies to 2 billion ringgit ($510 million) from 700 million ringgit to maintain the fixed price of the fuel.

THAILAND

** Thailand is considering cuts to its oil tax, its finance minister said.

** Thailand has discussed with the Russian government the possibility of purchasing crude oil, a deputy prime minister said on Tuesday. The minister also said the government would try to cap domestic diesel prices at 33 baht ($1.02) per litre.

** The Thai Planning Agency said the government will freeze prices of some goods and provide support for farmers.

PHILIPPINES

** The Philippines said it plans to curb power bills as LNG prices surge by boosting coal-fired power generation and regulating electricity tariffs.

BRAZIL

** Brazil's President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva signed a decree to eliminate federal taxes on diesel.

EGYPT

** Egypt has capped the price of unsubsidised bread sold in private bakeries.

ETHIOPIA

** Ethiopia has increased fuel subsidies.

data.title: Chevron El Segundo Isomax Unit Restarts, Boosting Output of Jet Fuel and High-Octane Gasoline — OPIS
data.description:
Chevron on Thursday said the Isomax unit at its 269,000 b/d El Segundo, Calif., refinery has resumed operations after a pipe failure led to a five-month shutdown.

Repairs to the unit, which produces jet fuel and high-octane gasoline, were completed ahead of schedule, the company said. The El Segundo plant supplies more than 35% of the jet fuel used by Southern California refineries and spot Los Angeles jet fuel prices shot higher following the outage.

After an October fire at the refinery disrupted jet fuel production, cash prices rose by about 30cts/gal as LA jet fuel's differential to the NYMEX strengthened by more than 58cts over the nine trading days that began on Oct. 2.

Market sources have said structural changes in the market have more closely tied West Coast jet fuel prices to the global market, adding that any international supply disruption would likely send prices in the region higher.

Chevron sources about 20% of El Segundo's crude oil supply from in-state production and the company recently signaled that it could increase its use of Venezuelan crude.

LA spot jet fuel prices have risen by 78% since Feb. 27 after the U.S. and Israel launched attacks against Iran on Feb. 28 and the subsequent closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

The implied price for March-delivered LA jet fuel were up nearly 21cts to $4.60/gal at about 1:45 p.m. ET Thursday.

California jet fuel stocks rose to 2.983 million bbl in the week ended Friday from 2.838 million bbl in the previous week, the California Energy Commission reported. And the Energy Information Administration on Wednesday estimated West Coast jet fuel holdings rose by 500,000 bbl to 11.6 million bbl in the week ended Friday.

EIA also reported that jet fuel imports averaged 128,000 b/d last week, up 44,000 b/d in the previous week. Cargoes into the region in March were sourced and secured before the war began.

OPIS reported earlier that the arb between Asia and the West Coast is closed as jet fuel prices in Asia have soared on the Middle East conflict.

Market sources they expect the full effect of the changing trade flows is expected to be felt starting in April.

April-delivered LA jet was indicated 65cts over NYMEX May ULSD contract, putting the price at $4.76/gal.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Bayan Raji, braji@opisnet.com and My Nguyen, mynguyen@opisnet.com; Editing by Jeffrey Barber, jbarber@opisnet.com

data.title: IFOS: Fertilizer supply shocks drive grain prices higher as global food security risks intensify
data.description:
Global fertilizer markets face severe disruptions from Middle East conflict, impacting ammonia, LNG, and sulfur supplies, with rising grain prices and concerns over food security. The company remains resilient due to North American sourcing and strong operational performance, while expanding in Brazil and advancing growth projects.

data.title: NGVT: Transformation focuses on core segments, $1B cash for growth, debt paydown, and buybacks
data.description:
The company is undergoing a major transformation, divesting non-core assets and focusing on two highly profitable segments: Performance Materials and Pavement Technologies. With $1 billion in deployable cash expected over two years, priorities include organic growth, debt reduction, and share repurchases.
